general.title: General
general.languageDescription: Switch MyAgents interface copy. This does not change AI reply language.
general.autostartDescription: Run MyAgents automatically when the system starts
general.saveFailedRetry: Failed to save. Please try again.
general.minimizeToTrayDescription: Minimize to the system tray when closing the window instead of quitting
general.forceWakeDescription: When enabled, this computer will not automatically sleep even when no AI task is running. Closing the lid still sleeps the computer and battery drains faster.

general.queueTurnDescription: Send the next queued message only after AI finishes the current turn
general.queueRealtimeDescription: AI reads messages while running and decides when to respond
general.notificationEnableDescription: Notify when AI finishes a task or needs your confirmation
general.notificationSoundDescription: Play a sound when system notifications appear

general.proxyAppliedHint: Changes are applied automatically to running sessions
general.proxyProbeFailed: Proxy check failed. Please try again later.
general.logsDescription: Export logs from the last 3 days for troubleshooting

general.proactiveDescription: Give AI 24/7 awareness and action, and add Channels such as Feishu or DingTalk to interact with you proactively.

general.disabledWithChannels: Proactive Agent Mode disabled. {{count}} channel(s) stopped.

basics.name: Name

basics.providerUnavailableHint: This provider has no API key or subscription login
basics.noProviders: No available providers yet. Add an API key or complete subscription login in Settings → Model Providers first.
basics.runtimeChanged: Switched to {{label}}. New tabs will use it.
basics.externalRuntimeNotice: This Agent workspace now uses {{runtime}}. Conversations from MyAgents or bound Channels will call the locally installed {{runtime}} directly, just like using it in a terminal. Provider settings, supported models, MCP tools, and permission rules are managed by {{runtime}} itself. Change them in that runtime's own settings.
basics.proxyMyAgentsHint: Use the proxy configured in MyAgents (default)
basics.proxyTerminalHint: Same as manually starting it in your computer's terminal, inheriting proxy variables exported by the shell
basics.reasoningSupportHint: Requires provider support; actual behavior depends on the provider
basics.noGlobalTools: No global MCP tools are enabled yet. Enable them in system settings first.

permission.autoDescription: Agent acts in the workspace and asks before using tools
permission.planDescription: Agent only researches information and discusses plans with you
permission.fullAgencyDescription: Agent has full autonomous permission without human confirmation

agentList.empty: No Agents yet. Upgrade a workspace to an Agent from workspace settings.

upgrade.title: Upgrade to Agent
upgrade.description: After upgrading this workspace to an Agent, you can add IM Channels such as Feishu, Telegram, or DingTalk, so AI can interact with users through multiple channels while sharing the same workspace and tool configuration.
upgrade.benefitShared: Multiple Channels share one workspace and MCP tool set
upgrade.benefitOverrides: Independent AI model and permission override configuration
upgrade.benefitUnified: Unified heartbeat and scheduled task management

workspaceSelect.title: Select workspace
workspaceSelect.description: Select a workspace that has not been upgraded and convert it to an Agent.
workspaceSelect.empty: All workspaces have been upgraded to Agents.

toolGroups.title: Tool groups
toolGroups.description: Choose which tool groups this plugin can use. Disabling unused groups can reduce token usage.

botRegistry.title: Channel
botRegistry.description: Connect these platforms as Agent Channels, so AI Agents can interact through instant messaging.
botRegistry.updatedWithFailedRestart: Updated to {{version}}, but {{count}} Channel(s) failed to restart. Restart them manually.
botRegistry.installFromNpm: Install an OpenClaw plugin from npm
botRegistry.howToAddDescription: Channels attach to Agents. Use either path below.
botRegistry.pathExistingTitle: Path 1: Upgrade an existing workspace to a proactive Agent
botRegistry.pathExistingDescription: Best when you already have a project folder and want to add IM chat capability to it.
botRegistry.pathNewTitle: Path 2: Create a new Agent workspace
botRegistry.pathNewDescription: Best when you want to start from scratch with a dedicated Agent for a Channel.
botRegistry.guideExistingStep1: Find the target workspace on Launcher, hover to reveal settings, then open Agent Settings.
botRegistry.guideExistingStep2: In the General tab, find Proactive Agent Mode and turn it on.
botRegistry.guideExistingStep3: After Channels appear, click Add, choose a platform, and configure credentials.
botRegistry.guideNewStep1: Click Add on Launcher, choose Add local folder or Create Agent from template, then enable proactive mode and add a Channel.
botRegistry.uninstallConfirmMessage: Uninstall "{{name}}"? Channels using this plugin will not be able to start.

playwright.title: Playwright Browser Settings

playwright.persistentModeDescription: Keeps full login state; only one conversation can use the browser at a time
playwright.isolatedModeDescription: Multiple conversations can use the browser concurrently; login state is shared through snapshots
playwright.persistentWarning: In persistent mode, only one conversation can use the browser at a time. Other conversations must wait.
playwright.loginStateDescription: Each conversation uses an isolated browser, with login state shared across conversations through cookie snapshots.

runtimeMissing.title: Runtime Missing
runtimeMissing.descriptionSuffix: to run. Install it before enabling the service.

cliTools.title: CLI Tools
cliTools.description: Command-line tools created and registered by AI. Every runtime and terminal can call them directly; the toggle controls whether AI can discover them automatically.
cliTools.registerPrompt: Use tool-creator to add this CLI tool to MyAgents Tools:
cliTools.copySuccess: Copied. Paste it into a chat with AI.
cliTools.enabledToast: Added to AI context. New sessions will see it.
cliTools.disabledToast: Hidden from AI context. The tool can still be called manually.
cliTools.envSaved: Environment variables saved. They take effect next time the tool starts.
cliTools.emptyDescription: Ask AI to turn repeated work into command-line tools that every session can use. Try saying:
cliTools.existingToolHint: Already have a CLI tool? Send this to AI: "{{prompt}}"
cliTools.existingToolLabel: Already have a CLI tool? Copy this and send it to AI:
cliTools.toggleDisabledTitle: Hidden: AI will not discover it automatically, but it can still be called manually
cliTools.deleteMessage: Remove "{{name}}" from the registry and delete its launcher from PATH. The tool directory will stay on disk. New sessions will no longer see this tool.

examples.mergeMarkdown: Turn "merge multiple Markdown files" into a CLI tool I can reuse
examples.ttsApi: Wrap the Volcano Engine text-to-speech API as a CLI tool

detail.envHint: If a value is missing, the tool exits with code 3 so AI can guide you to fill it in instead of failing silently.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.descriptionIntro: MyAgents supports the Claude Plugins protocol. A plugin can include skills, sub-agents, hooks, and MCP.
plugins.descriptionInstall: Use Install Plugin to add one from a GitHub link or local folder.
plugins.descriptionVisibilityPrefix: The plugin switch controls whether the plugin is
plugins.descriptionVisibilitySuffix: . To actually enable it, select it in Agent Settings or the Chat input tool menu.
plugins.emptyDescription: Use Install Plugin in the upper right to add one from GitHub or a local path.

toasts.hidden: Hidden {{name}}. It is no longer selectable in workspaces.
toasts.shown: Shown {{name}}. Enable it from Agent Settings or the Chat tool menu.

uninstall.title: Uninstall {{name}}?
uninstall.message: This will delete the plugin directory and remove it from the enabled list. The data directory ({{dataDir}}) is kept by default.

input.title: Install Plugin
input.localPathPrefix: links, and local folder absolute paths such as
input.trustWarning: Plugins run arbitrary code, start MCP processes, and trigger hook scripts with your user permissions. Install only trusted sources.

select.title: Select Plugins to Install
select.defaultAll: ; all valid plugins are selected by default.

helper.title: AI Helper
helper.placeholder: Tell the AI Helper what you want: configure models, add MCP, troubleshoot issues, send feedback, or attach a web link or screenshot. The helper can handle it for you.
helper.emptyTip: Enter what you want or attach an image
helper.feedbackEmptyTip: Describe the issue or add an image
helper.feedbackPlaceholder: Describe the issue you ran into, or share feedback and suggestions

floatingBallPet.title: Desktop Pet
floatingBallPet.description: Use a floating desktop pet to summon MyAgents at any moment, talk with AI, or delegate a task.
floatingBallPet.enableDescription: Show the floating desktop entry point and companion window.
floatingBallPet.workspaceBindingDescription: Follow the launcher workspace by default, or pin the pet to a specific project.
floatingBallPet.hoverPeekDescription: Automatically open a translucent preview when the cursor hovers over the floating ball. When off, clicking the ball still opens it.
floatingBallPet.petStyleDescription: Built-in styles and imported assets both use the Codex Pets 9-state spritesheet protocol, and the runtime switches animations based on the current Agent state.
floatingBallPet.dropImportTitle: Drag to import Codex Pets assets
floatingBallPet.dropImportDescription: Drop a folder containing pet.json and spritesheet.webp/png, or a Codex Pets .zip file. Imports validate the 9-state asset size and manifest references.
floatingBallPet.deleteDialogMessage:
Delete "{{name}}"?
This removes the asset from your local imported list.

petdexDialog.title: Import Petdex Link
petdexDialog.description: Paste a Petdex pet page link. MyAgents will download the zip package, validate it against the Codex Pets protocol, and import it.

errors.unsupportedDelete: This environment does not support deleting Desktop Pet assets

errors.unsupportedPetdexImport: This environment does not support Petdex link import

about.desktopOnly: This feature is only available in the desktop app
about.upToDate: You are already on the latest version
about.checkFailed: Failed to check for updates. Please try again later.
about.developerParagraph1: MyAgents is a desktop AI Agent client that lives on your computer: your personal AI center. It runs on the Claude Agent SDK, supports multiple model providers, and lets you switch quickly. Operations happen locally, and your data stays on your computer.
about.developerParagraph2: Claude Code let developers experience AI-amplified productivity first, while OpenClaw showed a glimpse of proactive agent assistants for everyone. MyAgents aims to make local agents complete: when you are at your computer, it can reach your files, projects, and tools to collaborate precisely and produce high-quality work; when you are away, it can act like your delegate, sensing the world 24/7 and continuing to work from your intent.
about.developerParagraph3: Unlike AI tools that need a fresh introduction every conversation, Agents in MyAgents sync deeply with your life and work and become partners that understand you better over time. We want it to become a super-amplifier for everyone's intent:
about.developerQuote: You have an idea. And it's done.
about.runtimeDescription: Enable external runtimes such as Claude Code CLI and Codex CLI in the input box and Agent settings. Disable this to use the built-in runtime only.
about.cliRegistryDescription: Allow AI to create and register command-line tools, then manage them in Tools. New sessions can discover enabled tools automatically. MCP and built-in myagents CLI capabilities are not affected.
about.desktopPetDescription: Mino's desktop pet: a floating ball that stays near the screen edge, lets you glance on hover, click to ask, and lets the pet keep running after you send. Once enabled, you can manage it from Settings.
about.helperDescription: The AI Helper can analyze local logs to answer questions, report issues, or send suggestions
about.communityDescription: Scan the QR code to join and talk with other users

developer.devModeDescription: Show log entry buttons on pages, such as Logs and System Info.
developer.codexProviderDescription: Show the MyAgents-managed Codex subscription entry on the Model Providers page.
developer.teamAvailable: Show the Team tab in the title bar. This feature is still in development, off by default, and already-open team pages are hidden when disabled.
developer.teamUnavailable: This build does not include the team service. Build with MYAGENTS_SPACE_ENABLED=true and MYAGENTS_SPACE_BASE_URL to enable it.
developer.teamUnavailableWithReason: This build does not include the team service. Build with MYAGENTS_SPACE_ENABLED=true and MYAGENTS_SPACE_BASE_URL to enable it. ({{reason}})
developer.splitViewDescription: Open file previews in a right-side split view instead of a modal.
developer.backgroundAgentPermissionSuffix: cannot show permission dialogs. Inherit approved only allows tools you have already marked Always Allow; others are denied with a hint. Full auto lets Background Agents use all non-interactive tools, which is broader and should be used carefully.
developer.liteLlmRefreshDescription: Periodically fetch the LiteLLM model registry from GitHub as fallback data for model context lengths, with a startup check and daily incremental updates.
developer.eagerForkDescription: Use the SDK's separate forkSession() path when forking sessions, creating the fork immediately with UUID remapping. Disable to use the stable legacy path.
developer.claudeTranscriptRetentionDescription: Writes cleanupPeriodDays to the Claude Agent SDK to control local transcript auto-cleanup.
developer.cronTaskDescription: View and manage running recurring tasks for development debugging.
